_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging global prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with 2015 as the base year (100), now stands at 141, indicating a roughly 41% rise since 2015. This headline increase has not been steady over time but rather shows recent and uneven gains across components. It reflects a composite measure rather than a blanket inflation across all commodities in the basket. Such an index helps track broad trends in key breakfast-related soft commodities worldwide.
notes.movers: Coffee has been the primary driver with a 99% increase since 2015, followed by orange juice up 34%, wheat up 19%, and sugar up 13%. These changes are largely attributed to specific weather events and crop diseases—such as drought and frost impacts on arabica coffee regions and citrus greening coupled with hurricanes affecting orange juice supplies. These soft commodity markets are relatively thin, meaning they can experience significant price swings from localized shocks rather than general demand pressures or broad inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in the basket is fundamentally rooted in supply disruptions to a few weather-sensitive crops rather than systemic inflation across all commodities. Because such environmental factors can persist or recur, price levels in these agricultural inputs may remain elevated for extended periods. This dynamic underscores the sensitivity of thin soft commodity markets to climatic and biological risks.
